reflection.quote: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared to what lies within us.
reflection.reading:
Recovery rarely arrives as a single grand decision. It arrives in small ones, made over and over, on days that look ordinary from the outside. The choice to put the bottle down. The choice to make the call. The choice to go to the meeting even when the couch feels safer.

What is being built underneath those choices is not visible yet. It feels, most days, like nothing is happening. But the foundation of a sober life is laid in exactly this kind of unremarkable repetition. The morning you make coffee instead of pouring something else. The walk you take when the urge comes. The honest sentence you say out loud to one person who knows you.

If today feels too small to count, that is the work. The unglamorous, faithful, daily turning toward yourself. You are doing it. Even now.
reflection.meditation: Today I will trust that small acts of care are not nothing — they are the whole thing. I will let one ordinary good choice be enough.

affirmations: I am building a life I do not need to escape from, one day at a time.
Today my sobriety is not a punishment — it is a gift I am learning to receive.
I trust the version of me that wants to heal more than the version that wants to numb.

gratitude.prayer: For this morning, and the chance to begin again, I am grateful. For the people who have stayed, for the body that is still here, for the small ordinary things that I once could not see — coffee, breath, weather, light through a window — thank you. May I carry this gratitude into the harder hours of the day.
